questions.question: Rearrange the following parts to form a meaningful and grammatically correct sentence: P. that maintaining a positive attitude Q. even in difficult situations R. is essential for success S. and helps overcome obstacles effectively
questions.explanation: The meaningful order is: that maintaining a positive attitude even in difficult situations is essential for success and helps overcome obstacles effectively.

questions.question: Choose the option that best completes the sentence: “She was so tired she could hardly keep her eyes open.”
questions.explanation: The correct construction is so tired that she could hardly keep her eyes open.

questions.question: Choose the correct option to complete the sentence: “Neither the students nor the teacher ready for the exam.”
questions.explanation: With neither...nor, the verb agrees with the nearer subject teacher, so is is correct.

questions.question: Choose the option that best completes the sentence: “If I enough money, I would buy a new car.”
questions.explanation: The second conditional takes the past form: If I had enough money, I would buy a new car.

questions.question: Rearrange the following parts to form a meaningful and grammatically correct sentence: P. the teacher explained the lesson clearly Q. so that every student could understand it R. during the online class yesterday S. with examples and illustrations
questions.explanation: The sentence reads: during the online class yesterday, the teacher explained the lesson clearly with examples and illustrations so that every student could understand it.

questions.question: Rearrange the following parts to form a meaningful and grammatically correct sentence: P. a healthy diet and regular exercise Q. are important habits R. that help maintain good physical and mental health S. especially in today’s busy world
questions.explanation: The sentence reads: a healthy diet and regular exercise are important habits that help maintain good physical and mental health, especially in today’s busy world.

questions.question: Choose the option that best explains the meaning of the idiom in the sentence: “After working hard all year, she finally decided to and take a long vacation.”
questions.explanation: To hit the road means to leave or start a journey.

questions.question: Choose the option that best completes the sentence with the correct phrase: “He was feeling very nervous before the exam, but he managed to and do well.”
questions.explanation: To keep his chin up means to stay positive and composed despite nervousness.

questions.question: Choose the word that is a synonym of the underlined word: “The teacher was very benevolenttowards her students.”
Choose the word that is an antonym of the underlined word: “His answer was very accurate.”
questions.explanation: The antonym of accurate is wrong.

questions.question: Choose the word that is a synonym of the underlined word: “The scientist made a significant discovery.”
Choose the word that is an antonym of the underlined word: “She was very generous with her time and money.”
questions.explanation: The antonym of generous is selfish.

questions.question: Choose the option that best completes the sentence: “Despite the heavy rain, the match without any delay.”
questions.explanation: Despite the rain, the match continued without delay.

questions.question: Choose the option that best completes the sentence: “If you want to improve your English, you practice regularly.”
questions.explanation: Must expresses necessity, so it best completes the advice to practice regularly.
